title: spergularia urbica
intro: Spergularia urbica, also known as sand-spurrey, is a perennial weed plant that belongs to the family Caryophyllaceae and is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Spergularia urbica include its ability to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Herbal preparations of Spergularia urbica are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
